evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: uses securegw-stage.paytm.in, /theia/api/v1/initiateTransaction, paytmchecksum library, and CheckoutJS bridge pattern — all consistent with known Paytm All-in-One flow. No version claim, but self-consistent across backend/frontend.
evidence.executability: Code blocks parse. Issues: PAYTM_MID hardcoded as 'YOUR_TEST_MID' on frontend (not from env), missing .env template file, no onload handler on script (acknowledged in pitfalls but not fixed in code). Test command not in fenced bash block following a Test header. No sandbox-infeasible commands.
evidence.completeness: (a) acquisition steps present; (b) install commands present; (c) env vars referenced (PAYTM_MID, PAYTM_MERCHANT_KEY) but NO explicit .env template block — listed only in prose; (d) backend code present; (e) frontend code present; (f) test procedure described but no single runnable test command in a bash block. Env var mismatch: frontend hardcodes MID instead of using env. Cap at 3.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Two paytm links cited (dashboard.paytm.com, business.paytm.com/docs/test-instruments). business.paytm.com/docs is the canonical official domain. No deep link to initiateTransaction docs near the relevant backend code.

rubric_notes: Long-tail handling applied (docs_unavailable). Playbook is internally consistent with no version claim → CF=4. No explicit .env template block and frontend hardcodes MID → completeness capped at 3.
